key_insight: This study will build and validate multi-modal pregnancy risk models using biomarkers, ultrasound, and maternal data to better predict stillbirth and related conditions in high-burden settings.
clinical_relevance: If successful, the models could help identify high-risk pregnancies earlier, supporting more targeted monitoring and potentially reducing preventable stillbirths and unnecessary early birth.
method: Prospective multi-centre cohort across four high-volume referral hospitals in Uganda.
Enroll at least 6,075 pregnant women and collect data at multiple gestational timepoints.
Use clinical, hemodynamic, ultrasound, biochemical, and placental histopathology data to develop regression and machine-learning models.
evidence_signal: Model performance will be assessed with calibration plots, precision, recall, F1 score, and accuracy, with validation planned in the study design.
watch_item: Implementation may be limited if the resulting models are too resource-intensive or if missing data and local workflow constraints reduce real-world usability.
paper_title: Reducing stillbirth in high burden settings using biomarkers and ultrasound technologies: protocol for the multi-centre prospective iTECH cohort study
